SEARCH
Package gov.cdc.izgw.v2tofhir.terminology

Class TerminologyMapperFactory

java.lang.Object
gov.cdc.izgw.v2tofhir.terminology.TerminologyMapperFactory
public final class TerminologyMapperFactory extends Object
Factory that provides the application-wide TerminologyMapper instance.

On the first call to get() the factory reads the environment variable V2TOFHIR_TERMINOLOGY_MAPPER. If the variable is set, the factory attempts to load the named class and instantiate it via its public no-argument constructor. The class must implement TerminologyMapper; if it only implements a sub-interface the factory treats it as an error and falls back to the default.

If the variable is absent, the named class cannot be found, does not implement TerminologyMapper, has no public no-arg constructor, or throws during construction, the factory logs a warning and falls back to DefaultTerminologyMapper. The application continues to function normally in all error cases.

The resolved instance is cached in an AtomicReference; subsequent calls to get() return the same object reference without re-reading the environment variable or repeating class loading.

Author:
Audacious Inquiry
See Also:

  • DefaultTerminologyMapper

  • Field Details

    • ENV_VAR

      public static final String ENV_VAR
      Environment variable that names the TerminologyMapper implementation to use.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • get

      public static TerminologyMapper get()
      Returns the application-wide TerminologyMapper instance, creating it on first call.

      Resolution order:

      1. Return cached instance if already resolved.
      2. Read V2TOFHIR_TERMINOLOGY_MAPPER; if set, attempt dynamic load.
      3. Fall back to DefaultTerminologyMapper on any error or when the variable is absent.
      Returns:
      the shared TerminologyMapper; never null

    • setDefaultSupplier

      public static void setDefaultSupplier(Supplier<TerminologyMapper> supplier)
      Registers a supplier to use as the default TerminologyMapper when V2TOFHIR_TERMINOLOGY_MAPPER is not set.

      Downstream libraries that embed v2tofhir and provide their own TerminologyMapper extension should call this during their own initialization so that get() returns their implementation without requiring the end-user to configure any environment variable.

      If get() has already resolved and cached an instance this method is a no-op (with a WARN log entry). The last supplier set before the first get() call takes effect; use resetForTesting() in tests to restore a clean state.

      Parameters:
      supplier - a non-null supplier that produces the desired default mapper
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if supplier is null
